description: Transactions per second (TPS) is a measure of a computer system's (or network's) capacity to perform transactions or calculations in a second. TPS is the rate at which a system can process transactions, i.e., at which rate blocks can be created on the blockchain.

title: TRC-10 (TRON)
description: TRC10 is a technological token standard that does not require the TRON Virtual Machine and is backed by the TRON blockchain network (TVM).

title: TRC-20 Token
description: The TRC-20 token standard allows for tokens to be created on the TRON network.

title: Treasury Bills (T-Bills)
description: Treasury bills (T-Bills) are short-term U.S. government debt obligations.

title: Treasury Bond (T-Bond)
description: The treasury bond is a debt security issued by the United States government that is backed by the 'full faith and credit' of the U.S. Treasury Department.

title: Trojan
description: A trojan is a type of malware that is often disguised as legitimate software.

title: Truffle
description: For blockchains using EVM, truffle acts as a development environment and a testing framework, a widely used tool for blockchain application development.

title: Trust
description: A trust is a fiduciary relationship in which one party, known as a trustor, gives another party, the trustee, the right to hold title to property or assets for the benefit of a third party, the beneficiary.

title: Trust Wallet
description: Trust Wallet is a crypto wallet software that gives its users complete ownership of their crypto funds.

title: Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs)
description: Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) are secure areas within a main processor that provide a protected space where sensitive code and data can operate without fear of tampering or observation from the outside world.

title: Trusted Yield Environment (TYE)
description: Trusted Yield Environment (TYE) is a specialized DeFi security infrastructure that protects AI agents. It provides curated API access to blue-chip DeFi opportunities, helping agents who manage on-chain capital safely deploy their capital into vetted yield-generating protocols.

title: Trustless
description: An environment where there is no centralized authority.

title: Tumbler
description: A mixing service that helps make cryptocurrency funds and transactions more anonymous.

title: Turing Completeness
description: Turing completeness refers to the capability of a system or programming language to solve any problem that can be solved by a machine created by mathematician Alan Turing.

title: Turing-Complete
description: A system that in principle could be able to solve any computation problem. Turing complete is a term given to a system that is able to recognize or decide specific other data-manipulation rule sets (the ones used by Turing Machines); Turing complete is a label used to express the power of such a data-manipulation rule set; the large majority of modern programming languages are Turing complete.

title: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
description: Two-factor authentication (2FA) is method of access that requires two different forms of authentication.

title: Type Checking
description: A process in programming languages to verify whether or not each operation in the program is in accordance with the type declaration rules of the language.

title: TypeScript Programming Language
description: TypeScript programming language is a more advanced version of JavaScript that includes more functionality.

title: Typosquatting
description: Typosquatting is a deceptive practice used by scammers to trick people into entering a counterfeit website and compromising their private information.

title: Unbanked
description: Unbanked refers to those that are either unable to access banking services, or choose not to. Unbanked is a characteristic describing people who do not use banks or financial institutions oftentimes because they do not have access to banking services or because they prefer cash transactions outside the banking system.

title: Uncle Block (Ommer Block)
description: Uncle Block (Ommer Block) refers to the discarded block in the phenomenon when two blocks are simultaneously created, resulting in one block being omitted from the blockchain.

title: Unconfirmed
description: A state in which a transaction has not been appended to the blockchain. A zero confirmation or unconfirmed transaction is defined as an exchange that has not yet been recorded or verified on the blockchain.

title: UNI Token
description: A native governance token of Uniswap, the largest decentralized exchange at the time of writing.

title: Unit of Account
description: A unit of account is a standardized unit of measurement used in accounting to record and track financial transactions.

title: United States House Committee on Financial Services
description: The U.S. House Committee on Financial Services is a committee of the United States House of Representatives that oversees all components of the US’s financial and housing services.

title: Unpermissioned Ledger
description: A public ledger that is open to anyone, without being controlled by a single owner.

title: Unrealized Profit & Loss
description: Unrealized profit and loss occur when you have a position open in a security that has appreciated or depreciated in value.

title: Unregulated
description: Something that isn’t controlled by a centralized authority or a governing intuition.

title: Unspent Transaction Output (UTXO)
description: A transaction that is left unspent after being completed, similar to leftover change after making a purchase. Wallet balances in cryptocurrency networks are not actually stored as a variable, like John Doe’s balance: 100 BTC. Instead, they are calculated from several UTXOs, which are essentially units of unspent cryptocurrency left over from the sum of all transactions made on the wallet. Cumulatively, the sum of an address’s UTXOs make up that wallet’s available balance.

title: Unstoppable Domains
description: Unstoppable Domains is the name of a San-Francisco based company that provides blockchain-based domain names to users.

title: US Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC)
description: The US Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) is a financial intelligence and enforcement agency of the US Treasury Department.

title: Use Case
description: A use case is a description of the interactions between an actor (human or system) and a system that leads to an event.

title: Useful Proof of Work (UPoW)
description: UPoW channels computational power into training artificial intelligence (AI).

title: User Interface
description: The user interface is the user’s interaction with a website or application using a digital device.

title: UTC Time
description: Coordinated Universal Time.

title: Utility Mining
description: Utility mining is a mechanism that allows for the distribution of tokens based on user activity and active participation. This allows crypto projects to distribute flexible yields attached to any specified on-chain interaction.

title: Utility Token
description: Tokens that are designed specifically to be able to help people use something. A utility token is a token issued in order to fund the development of an IT system and/or business model and can typically be used to later on purchase goods or services created by the issuer of that token and can thus oftentimes be seen as usage rights.

title: Valhalla in Crypto
description: In crypto, Valhalla represents the promised land - that moment when your diamond hands finally pay off and you achieve financial freedom.

title: Validator
description: A participant on a proof-of-stake (PoS) blockchain, involved in validating blocks for rewards. Validators are participants in a blockchain network who verify incoming transactions. Used in proof-of-stake platforms instead of miners, validators help maintain the integrity of a blockchain and keep it secure, receiving rewards for their work. Validators are chosen by the network to validate new blocks based on the amount of cryptocurrency they have staked in the network.

title: Vanity Address
description: A cryptocurrency public address with custom letters and numbers, usually picked by its owner.

title: Vaporware
description: A cryptocurrency project that is never actually developed.

title: Venture Capital
description: A form of private equity provided to fund small, early-stage firms considered to have high growth potential.

title: Verifiable Internet
description: The goal of the Verifiable Internet is to eliminate the cost and expense of confirming the authenticity of digital data, and to have proof of the verification easily transferred as the data moves from place to place.

title: Verification Code
description: A verification code is a security protection method that is used to avoid internet bots from abusing or even spamming various online services.

title: Vesting Period
description: The act of restricting the sale of a token for a particular period of time. Vesting is a term characterizing the fact that an asset is wholly owned by an investor; crypto projects oftentimes implement long-lasting token release vesting schedules for their token sale investors, locking them into those tokens for a considerable period of time.

title: Virgin Bitcoin
description: A Bitcoin that has never been spent.

title: Virtual Automated Market Makers (vAMMs)
description: A virtual Automated Market Maker (vAMM) is a system that provides synthetic (or virtual) liquidity, allowing traders to buy and sell derivatives entirely on the blockchain.

title: Virtual Commodity Association (VCA)
description: The virtual commodity association is a non-profit organization of cryptocurrency exchanges and custodians with an aim to build a self-regulatory organization (SRO) for the U.S. virtual currency industry.

title: Virtual Private Network (VPN)
description: A virtual private network (VPN) is a technology that creates a safe and encrypted network from a public internet connection – giving you anonymity and privacy.

title: Virtual Reality (VR)
description: Virtual reality (VR) technology is used to simulate an immersive artificial world that can mimic or transcend reality.

title: Virus
description: Computers are usually infected with a virus when a user unknowingly installs it via a downloaded file.

title: Vitalik Buterin
description: Vitalik Buterin is one of the creators of Ethereum, the second-largest cryptocurrency after Bitcoin. Vitalik Buterin is a Russian-Canadian developer best known as a co-founder of Ethereum.

title: Volatility
description: A statistical measure of dispersion of returns, measured by using the standard deviation or variance between returns from that same security or market index. The opposite of stability, volatility refers to an asset's tendency to vary in price. Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies are notoriously volatile, which is to be expected for such a young market and can be the main appeal for crypto traders.

title: Volume
description: How much cryptocurrency has been traded over a set period, such as the past 24 hours. Trading volume refers to the total number of shares (or tokens/coins) that have been exchanged between buyers and sellers of a given asset during trading hours of a certain day.

title: WAGMI
description: WAGMI stands for 'We're All Gonna Make It.' It's a phrase about positivity, optimism, and a collective belief in success.

title: Wallet
description: A place where cryptocurrency users can store, send, and receive digital assets. Wallets are tools used by crypto holders to control their private keys and provide an interface to make transactions. Both software and hardware wallets are available today, and wallets can be custodial or non-custodial. The distinction here is whether or not the user retains full control of their private key. See digital wallet.

title: Wallet Score
description: A Bayesian statistical modeling system that assigns daily scores to wallet addresses based on user activity across blockchain ecosystems, where higher wallet scores demonstrate a statistical relationship with increased market capitalization.

title: Wallstreetbets (WSB)
description: Wallstreetbets, otherwise known as /r/wallstreetbets or WSB, is a subreddit for participants to discuss stock and options trading.

title: WannaCry Ransomware
description: WannaCry is a piece of ransomware that can infect and spread rapidly through a number of computer networks.

title: Wasabi Wallet
description: Wasabi is an open-source and privacy-focused wallet for Bitcoin, available for Windows, Linus, and macOS.

title: Wash Trade
description: A form of market manipulation in which investors create artificial activity in the marketplace by simultaneously selling and buying the same cryptocurrencies. A wash trade is a trade on an exchange used to manipulate the market: An investor simultaneously sells and buys the identical financial instruments in order to create exaggerated and potentially misleading activity in the marketplace. Wash trades are often used by cryptocurrency exchanges to inflate trading volumes.

title: Watchdog Organization
description: Non-profit organizations that critically monitor the activities of governments or other entities on behalf of the public.

title: Watcher (OMG Foundation)
description: A Watcher on OMG Foundation is a computer that observes the child chain and block producer and makes sure network transactions are correctly confirmed.

title: Watchlist
description: A watchlist is a feature of the website where users can create their own lists of cryptocurrencies to follow.

title: Weak Hands
description: An investor prone to panic selling at the first sign of a price decline. Weak hands is a term used to describe investors selling their assets during a negative market sentiment, similar to a card player who folds their cards at the first sign that they are going to lose; typically, weak hands will sell during lows.

title: Web 1.0
description: Web 1.0 is a term that is often used to describe the early version of the internet.

title: Web 2.0
description: Web 2.0 describes the current state of the web, which supports more user-generated content and stability for front-end users than its predecessor, Web 1.0.

title: Web 3.0
description: Web 3.0 is the coming generation of the internet.

title: Web3 Foundation
description: The Web3 Foundation was created to foster new technologies and applications in the field of decentralized web software protocols.

title: WebSocket
description: WebSocket enables two-way communication between a client (e.g., a web browser) and a server (which can be either an HTTP server or another WebSocket server).

title: Wei
description: The smallest fraction of an Ether, with each Ether to 1000000000000000000 Wei. Wei is the smallest denomination of Ether and a measurement unit of Gas. 1 Ether = 1,000,000,000,000,000,000 Wei.

title: Whale
description: A term used to describe investors who have uncommonly large amounts of crypto, especially those with enough funds to manipulate the market. Whale is a term used to describe participants in the cryptocurrency market that are large enough to influence it; in this context, the ocean serves as a metaphor for the entire market as it is home to large and small fish and waves can be understood as market movements. A 'whale' is a loose slang term used to describe big players in the cryptocurrency markets, from institutional investors to hedge funds or wealthy individuals.

title: What Is the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN)?
description: The Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) is a U.S. bureau that analyzes financial transactions to prevent financial crimes.

title: When Lambo
description: A phrase referring to when cryptocurrency holders will become rich enough to afford the purchase of a Lamborghini.

title: When Moon
description: A phrase used to ask when the price of cryptocurrencies will explode.

title: White Hat Computer Hacker
description: White hat hackers use their skills to improve security by exposing vulnerabilities before malicious hackers (known as black hat hackers) can detect and exploit them.

title: White Label
description: White Labeling allows a company to customize an existing product framework in order to rebrand and resell this product as their own.

title: White Label Staking
description: In white-label staking, the crypto holders get their validator node explicitly created for them. This is then managed entirely on their behalf by a third-party operator.

title: White Swan Event
description: A white swan is an easily predictable event that is anticipated based on current information and forecasting.

title: Whitelist
description: A list of interested participants in an initial coin offering, who registered their intent to take part or purchase in a sale. Whitelisting is the process of explicitly allowing specific identified investors to participate in a token sale.

title: Whitepaper
description: A document released by a crypto project that gives investors technical information about its concept, and a roadmap for how it plans to grow and succeed. A white paper is an in-depth report that aims to educate the reader on a particular issue, or explain a particular methodology or solution; in the crypto-world, white paper often refers to the document issued by companies prior to their ICO or STO, informing the reader of the purpose of the ICO or STO and the technology behind it, in which case the white paper could be argued to be a tool to help the company sell the cryptocurrencies. White papers are documents that are typically academic in nature, proposing a new technology and outlining the exact details of its implementation. Typically, new projects will launch white papers to help potential users or investors understand the product or service, its use case, and its potential.

title: Winding Down
description: Winding down is the process of unwrapping tokens back to their original form in DeFi.

title: Winding Up
description: In decentralized finance (DeFi), winding up essentially means wrapping crypto tokens through various projects in order to find the best yield.

title: x86 Virtual Machine (Qtum)
description: X86 Virtual Machine enables Qtum developers to write smart contracts in a language of their choice.

title: XBT
description: XBT is the lesser-known ticker symbol for Bitcoin.

title: Yield Curve
description: The yield curve is a line that plots the relationship between yields and maturities of fixed income securities.

title: Yield Farming
description: Yield farming involves earning interest by investing crypto in decentralized finance markets.

title: Yield Sensitivity
description: Yield sensitivity or interest rate sensitivity is a measure of how much a fixed income asset’s price changes due to the fluctuation in interest rates.

title: YTD
description: Stands for Year to Date.

title: Zero Confirmation Transaction
description: Alternative phrasing for an unconfirmed transaction.

title: Zero Confirmation/Unconfirmed Transaction
description: A zero confirmation or unconfirmed transaction is defined as an exchange that has not yet been recorded or verified on the blockchain.

title: Zero Knowledge Proof
description: Proving certain information or data is true without revealing it. In cryptography, a zero-knowledge proof enables one party to provide evidence that a transaction or event happened without revealing private details of that transaction or event.

title: Zero Knowledge Rollup
description: A zero knowledge rollup is a type of layer 2 scaling solution that relies on zero knowledge cryptography. A zero-knowledge rollup is a Layer 2 blockchain solution that performs computations and storage off-chain while funds are held in a smart contract.

title: Zero Knowledge Rollups
description: A zero-knowledge rollup is a Layer 2 blockchain solution that performs computations and storage off-chain while funds are held in a smart contract.

title: Zero-Knowledge (ZK) Hardware
description: Zero-Knowledge (ZK) hardware refers to processors specially designed to accelerate Zero-Knowledge proofs, which verify actions and transactions on the blockchain without exposing underlying data.

title: Zero-Knowledge Proof
description: In cryptography, a zero-knowledge proof enables one party to provide evidence that a transaction or event happened without revealing private details of that transaction or event.

title: ZK Coprocessor
description: A ZK Coprocessor functions as an auxiliary processing unit that operates alongside a blockchain's main network. Its primary role is to execute complex computations off-chain and subsequently generate zero-knowledge proofs that attest to the correctness of these computations.

title: zkApps
description: zkApps leverage the zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs), particularly zk-SNARKs and zk-STARKs, to unlock a realm of possibilities that surpass traditional blockchain applications.

title: ZKML (Zero-Knowledge Machine Learning)
description: ZKML enables verification of ML models while keeping data private through zero-knowledge proofs.

title: zkOracle
description: zkOracles utilize Zero Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) as a trustless medium through which data can pass freely whilst retaining a robust standard of security, privacy, and cost-efficiency.

title: zkSharding
description: zkSharding is a re-introduced concept of Ethereum’s sharded execution layer which scales blockchains with parallelized execution within shards secured via zkEVM proofs.

title: zParachain
description: A zParachain is an approach to bridging where a dedicated blockchain in the broader Polkadot ecosystem is used specifically to connect to a network that’s outside the Polkadot ecosystem.

title: ZK-SNARKs
description: A proof that allows one party to prove it owns certain information without revealing it.
